channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Geopolitical news headlines from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇨🇳🇸🇦 China Calls for Hormuz to Stay Open in Xi-Bin Salman Call
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping urged Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman to maintain normal ship passage through the Strait of Hormuz, as Beijing steps up efforts to end the Iran war.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 US-Iran Peace Talks Set to Resume as Ceasefire Deadline Looms
item.description: Vice President JD Vance is expected to travel to Pakistan for the second round of peace negotiations, though Iran has not confirmed its participation as the two-week ceasefire nears its end.
item.dc:creator: New York Times

item.title: 🛢️🇺🇸 Trump Invokes Defense Production Act to Expand US Petroleum Output
item.description: President Trump signed a memorandum calling for expanded domestic petroleum production, refining and logistics capacity, citing national defense needs amid the ongoing Iran conflict.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🌊🇦🇪 Gulf States Fear Iran Talks May Cement Tehran Hormuz Control
item.description: Gulf countries worry that US-Iran negotiations might only secure reopening of the Strait of Hormuz while leaving Tehran with enhanced leverage over the critical waterway.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🍎 Apple Names John Ternus as CEO, Tim Cook Becomes Executive Chairman
item.description: Apple appointed insider John Ternus as its next CEO, with Tim Cook transitioning to executive chairman in a major leadership shakeup at the worlds most valuable company.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇺🇸 Trump Sends Mixed Signals on Iran War Path as Ceasefire Nears End
item.description: President Donald Trump gave mixed messages on the path ahead for the U.S. war with Iran, with the ceasefire set to expire Wednesday and prospects for extension described as "highly unlikely."
item.dc:creator: AP News

item.title: 🇧🇬 Pro-Russian Ex-Fighter Pilot Rumen Radev Sweeps Bulgaria Election
item.description: Former Bulgarian President Rumen Radev won a landslide victory in parliamentary elections, crushing long-dominant political forces and potentially pushing the EU and NATO member closer to Moscow.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukrainian Drone Strike on Russian Port of Tuapse Kills One
item.description: A Ukrainian drone attack on Russia's Black Sea port of Tuapse sparked a fire and killed at least one person, Russian officials said, the second such attack on the oil terminal in five days.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇯🇵 BOJ Likely to Hold Rates Steady Amid Middle East Uncertainty
item.description: The Bank of Japan is likely to hold off raising interest rates next week as fading prospects of a near-term end to the Middle East war keep the country's economic and price outlook highly uncertain.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇭🇺 Hungary Election Winner Peter Magyar Names Finance and Foreign Ministers
item.description: Hungarian election winner Peter Magyar nominated key ministers including Andras Karman as finance minister and Anita Orban as foreign minister for his incoming government.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇰🇷🇺🇸 South Korea-US tensions flare over North Korea intelligence leak
item.description: Seoul's Unification Minister Chung Dong-young rejects accusations he leaked sensitive information about North Korea's nuclear facilities, straining alliance coordination on Pyongyang policy.
item.dc:creator: SCMP

item.title: 🇨🇳🇪🇺 China threatens retaliation against EU firms over cybersecurity plans
item.description: Beijing warns of broad retaliation if Huawei, ZTE and other Chinese companies are penalized under proposed EU cybersecurity regulations, escalating tech tensions.
item.dc:creator: SCMP

item.title: 🇱🇧💔 UN reports 350,000 displaced and 177 children killed in Lebanon since March
item.description: UN peacekeepers continue observing Israeli artillery fire in southern Lebanon as humanitarian crisis deepens with over 1 million displaced facing worsening conditions.
item.dc:creator: Anadolu

item.title: 🇦🇫🇪🇺 EU to host Taliban officials for talks on deporting Afghans
item.description: Taliban officials expected in Brussels for negotiations on returning Afghan migrants to their home country, marking significant diplomatic engagement with the unrecognized government.
item.dc:creator: France 24

item.title: 🇷🇴⚠️ Romania plunges into political turmoil as Social Democrats move to topple PM
item.description: Prime Minister Ilie Bolojan faces ouster after coalition partners accuse him of destroying their social base with austerity measures, deepening Europe's governance instability.
item.dc:creator: Politico

item.title: 🇯🇵 Japan issues tsunami warnings after 7.7 magnitude earthquake strikes northern coast
item.description: A powerful 7.7 earthquake hit off northern Japan, triggering tsunami warnings and evacuation orders. Meteorological agency warns of possible stronger aftershocks in the coming week.
item.dc:creator: AP News

item.title: 🇨🇦 Canadian PM Carney says economic ties with US are weakness that must be corrected
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney announced Canada must diversify trade relationships and reduce dependence on the US economy, signaling a major shift in Canadian economic policy amid tensions.
item.dc:creator: AP News

item.title: 🇳🇿 New Zealand declares state of emergency as severe floods hit Wellington region
item.description: Widespread flooding in Wellington has submerged vehicles, uprooted trees and triggered landslides. State of emergency declared as cleanup begins.
item.dc:creator: BBC

item.title: 🚨 Around 250 Rohingya refugees missing after boat capsizes in Andaman Sea
item.description: A vessel carrying Rohingya refugees capsized in the Andaman Sea, with approximately 250 people reported missing in the latest tragedy involving the persecuted minority.
item.dc:creator: The Guardian

item.title: 🇮🇳 At least 21 killed as bus crashes into Kashmir gorge
item.description: An overcrowded bus skidded off a mountain road and fell into a gorge in Indian-administered Kashmir, killing at least 21 passengers and injuring dozens more.
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera

item.title: 🛢️ Oil Prices Surge 6% as US-Iran Ceasefire Falters, Stocks Retreat from Records
item.description: Global oil prices jumped 6% on fears the US-Iran ceasefire may collapse after the US Navy seized an Iranian cargo ship. Wall Street stocks pulled back from record highs as investors assessed the geopolitical risk.
item.dc:creator: AP News

item.title: 🇺🇸 Trump Says Not Under Pressure on Iran Deal as Ceasefire Deadline Looms
item.description: President Trump stated he faces no pressure to rush an Iran deal despite the ceasefire expiring Wednesday. Iran says it has no plans for the next round of negotiations, casting doubt on Islamabad peace talks.
item.dc:creator: CBS News

item.title: ⚔️ Iran Vows Retaliation After US Seizes Cargo Ship, Peace Talks in Doubt
item.description: Iran condemned the US Navy seizure of an Iranian-flagged tanker as armed piracy and vowed retaliation. The incident has cast doubt on whether the second round of peace talks in Islamabad will proceed.
item.dc:creator: NBC News

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine Officer Deploys 343 Days on Front Line, Highlighting Manpower Crisis
item.description: A Ukrainian infantry officer spent 343 consecutive days on the front line, believed to be one of the longest continuous combat deployments. The case underscores Kyivs growing manpower challenges after years of war.
item.dc:creator: CNN

item.title: 🇵🇱🇫🇷 Poland and France Discuss Joint Nuclear Exercises as US Commitment Wavers
item.description: Polish PM Donald Tusk and French President Macron discussed joint nuclear deterrence exercises. Poland is exploring deeper defense ties with France amid concerns about US commitment to European security.
item.dc:creator: Politico
